diagrams.title: OS System Model Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the layered architecture of an OS system model with user applications, system calls, kernel, and hardware
elements: Kernel with Process, Memory, Device, File managers
Hardware (CPU, Memory, I/O Devices) at bottom
Arrows showing interaction flow between layers
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how user programs interact with hardware through OS layers, understanding the role of system calls and kernel components

diagrams.title: Dual-Mode Operation Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates the transition between user mode and kernel mode using system calls and interrupts
elements: User Mode box with User Programs
Return path showing mode switch back
diagrams.purpose: Understand how the OS maintains protection by switching between privileged and non-privileged modes, ensuring secure hardware access

diagrams.title: Process State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the lifecycle of a process with various states and transitions
elements: Transitions: Admit, Dispatch, Interrupt, I/O wait, I/O complete, Exit
diagrams.purpose: Understand how the OS manages process execution through various states, critical for process management in system model
